Text: 340 customers from reddit. zero ad spend. everyone says reddit marketing is dead. they're wrong. the trick is not marketing at all. just be useful in the right threads and the traffic follows.

cold email reality check: ran 464k emails last year across clients. tested every list source out there. apollo, zoominfo, paid databases, manual scraping. buying lists is dead. here's what actually works.
inbox placement shifted from opens to engagement depth. reply rates doubled when we stopped tracking opens and started writing emails people actually respond to. stop spraying and praying.
intent-based timing gets 2-4x reply rates. send outreach when someone just posted a hiring tweet, just launched something, just complained about a problem you solve. timing is the whole game.
gmail now warns users about tracking pixels. if you're still using open tracking you're actively hurting your deliverability. disable it. focus on replies instead. the game changed.

what it actually costs to send 2500 cold emails daily: 10 domains at $12/yr each. 30 mailboxes at $4/mo each. validation at $0.003/email. sending platform $97/mo. total: under $300/mo. agencies charge $5-10k for this.
MCP multi-server automation. wired it into the stack this week. one instruction hits 6 different tools simultaneously. it's borderline illegal how much this saves you.
n8n for AI-native workflow automation. not the SaaS version. self-hosted. free. runs every pipeline we have. if you're paying for zapier you're leaving money on the table.
no-code browser automation via AI. the play: record a workflow once, the agent runs it 1000 times. price monitoring, lead scraping, form filling. zero code. just set it up.
1-bit LLMs for consumer-grade local inference. running full LLM pipelines on a laptop now. no API costs. no rate limits. no data leaving your machine. the cost curve just collapsed.

1-to-20 repurposing: write one good thing. auto-distribute to 20 formats. twitter thread, linkedin post, youtube script, email, reddit comment, short video hook. AI handles the adaptation. volume without the grind.

TikTok creator rewards pays 10x more than AdSense per view in certain niches. sponsorships on top of that. the stacked model: platform revenue + brand deals + affiliate. most people only run one of the three.

TikTok algo update: completion rate threshold increased. follower-first testing now active. this means your first 200 followers determine whether your videos get distributed. quality of early audience matters more than quantity.

first hour determines TikTok distribution. post and get 10 real comments in 60 minutes. that's the active creator signal. the algo reads it and decides whether to push to cold audiences. everything happens in that first hour.

YouTube Shorts SEO now matters. the 50-60 sec sweet spot is real. longer = search indexing. shorter = feed distribution. pick your goal and optimize for it. most people post random lengths and wonder why growth is inconsistent.
18-22 Shorts per month is the optimal cadence. not 5. not 60. 18-22. trending audio in the first 5 seconds. this is the playbook right now. stop guessing.

IG DM shares are now the top priority metric. not likes. not comments. shares to DMs. the algorithm reads this as high-trust content and pushes it to similar audiences. design every post to be DM-share-worthy.

IG Originality Score penalizes TikTok reposts. always remove watermarks. post 3-5 times per week not daily. IG is actively filtering content farms right now. original content is getting a massive reach advantage.

AI CI/CD autonomous deployment. push code, agent tests it, stages it, deploys it, monitors for errors, rolls back if needed. zero human in the loop after the push. shipping velocity 3x when this is running.

the cold email stack nobody breaks down: 6 qualifying questions, all answered in under 100 words. what you do. who for. how. problem you solve. proof. ROI. if you can't answer all 6 in 100 words your offer is still fuzzy.
